  4. TDS/TCS Return Filing & Compliance

TDS/TCS Return Filing & Compliance in Coimbatore

Quarterly TDS returns (24Q, 26Q, 27Q, 27EQ), TCS compliance, lower-deduction certificates and Form 16/16A generation for Coimbatore employers and businesses.

TDS compliance is unforgiving on deadlines — late filing attracts a daily fee that adds up fast, and defaults show up directly on your TRACES compliance report. We manage quarterly TDS return filing, TCS compliance, and Form 16/16A generation for Coimbatore employers, contractors, and businesses making specified payments, keeping deduction, deposit, and return filing tightly aligned.

What’s included

  • Form 24Q (salary TDS) quarterly filing
  • Form 26Q (non-salary payments — contractors, professionals, rent) filing
  • Form 27Q (payments to non-residents) filing
  • Form 27EQ (TCS returns) for applicable sellers
  • Form 16 and Form 16A generation and issuance
  • Lower/nil deduction certificate (Form 13) application support
  • Correction returns for challan or PAN mismatches

Our process

  1. Deduction mapping — We map every payment category — salary, contractor, rent, professional fees, commission — to the correct TDS section and rate, since misclassification is the most common cause of default notices.
  2. Challan payment tracking — TDS deposited monthly (by the 7th, or 30th for March) is tracked and matched against deductions to avoid short-payment defaults.
  3. Quarterly return preparation — 24Q, 26Q, 27Q, or 27EQ is prepared from your deduction data and validated through the File Validation Utility before submission.
  4. TRACES reconciliation — We check the filed return against TRACES for PAN errors, challan mismatches, or short deductions, correcting them proactively rather than waiting for a default notice.
  5. Certificate issuance — Form 16 (annual, for salary) and Form 16A (quarterly, for non-salary) are generated from TRACES and issued to deductees.

Documents you’ll need

  • Salary register / payroll data, for Form 24Q
  • Contractor, professional fee, and rent payment ledger, for Form 26Q
  • TDS challans (ITNS 281) paid for the quarter
  • PAN details of all deductees
  • Lower deduction certificate, if obtained by any deductee
  • Previous quarter's filed return, for continuing clients

TDS/TCS Return Filing & Compliance across Coimbatore

We serve tds/tcs return filing & compliance clients across every part of Coimbatore — from RS Puram and Gandhipuram in the city centre to Saravanampatti and Thudiyalur on the growth corridors, and out to Podanur and Sulur on the industrial belt. Whether you're based in Peelamedu, Kuniyamuthur, or Saravanampatti, our team meets you where your business operates.

Frequently asked questions about TDS/TCS Return Filing & Compliance

What is the penalty for late filing of TDS returns?

₹200 per day under Section 234E until the return is filed, capped at the TDS amount, plus a separate penalty under Section 271H (₹10,000 to ₹1,00,000) if the delay exceeds one year or the return contains incorrect details.

What TDS rate applies to a Coimbatore business paying a contractor?

Generally 1% for individual/HUF contractors and 2% for other entities under Section 194C, provided the contractor has furnished a valid PAN; without PAN, the rate rises to 20%.

Do I need to deduct TDS on rent paid for office premises?

Yes, if the annual rent exceeds ₹2,40,000, TDS at 10% (2% for machinery/equipment rent) applies under Section 194-I; for individuals/HUFs not subject to audit paying rent above ₹50,000/month, Section 194-IB applies instead.

What is a lower/nil deduction certificate and how do I get one?

It's a certificate under Section 197 allowing tax deduction at a lower rate (or nil) where the deductee's actual tax liability is lower than the standard TDS rate would suggest; we file Form 13 with supporting income projections to obtain it.

My TDS return shows a default for PAN mismatch — how is this fixed?

We file a correction return on TRACES updating the correct PAN against the challan, which resolves the default and restores the deductee's credit in their Form 26AS.

Is TCS applicable to my business, and on what transactions?

TCS applies to specified transactions — sale of certain goods above prescribed limits, foreign remittances under LRS, overseas tour packages, and a few other categories — we assess whether any of your transactions attract it.

How often do I need to file TDS returns?

Quarterly — by 31 July, 31 October, 31 January, and 31 May for the respective quarters — followed by issuance of Form 16/16A to deductees within the prescribed period after filing.
